node.title: Design a Real Time Event Aggregation System

topic.title: Design a Real Time Event Aggregation System

post.content:
Keeping two raw tables: trips and bills, both are sharded by date ( unix_timestamp(date) % hashbucket) 
Trips table will have following schema:  Date, TripId, City
Bills table will have Date, BillId, TripId, Amount.

Use following query to get daily roll up for trip counts and amount:
select date, city, count(tripids), sum(amount) from trips as t inner join bills as b on t.date =b.date and t.tripId = b.tripid

The intermediate result (daily rollup) can be cached and reused to avoid the expensive query that hits the raw tables.

To de-dup, you can use a Bloom filter to reject records with pre-existing tripId and billIds.

The two streams could be pushed to Kafka cluster with single topic with partition key derived using tripId so that events related to individual trip always lands up in the same partition.
A consumer group then could be setup to consume events from this topic and store it into a database table having tripId as a unique key which will avoid duplicate insertions.

Let's assume that we are getting 10K events every second which means we could have at max 20K operations every second on the database which can be easilty supported by Postgres.
In terms of memory consumption, if each trip needs ~600 bytes to store the data. Each day we would need to store -> 600*10000 bytes = 6 Mb/day, 180 Mb/month, 64 Gb/year. Which is not a lot of data to store in Postgres.

If we see a degradation in the performance, we can then do a range based sharding with every month's data sitting in different shard and then each shared can be queried and the result can then be aggregated to get the final result.

To fetch the data for City Trip and TotalAmount with the below query :
SELECT city, count(*), SUM(amount) FROM trip_table group by city;
